title: Pouteria gutta
intro: Pouteria gutta, also known as the Burut tree, is a tropical evergreen species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The health benefits of Pouteria gutta include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria gutta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pouteria gutta include extracts, teas, and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pouteria gutta is classified as a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, which consists of about 100 genera and 1,000 species of evergreen trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Sapotaceae family is part of the order Ebenales and is closely related to the family Ebenaceae. Pouteria gutta is further divided into several subspecies, each with distinct characteristics and distribution areas. The classification of Pouteria gutta is a subject of ongoing research and revision in the field of botany. This ongoing classification effort helps to better understand the relationships within the Sapotaceae family and its place in the larger plant kingdom.
